arts.ttl: Laura Woods surprised with 'amazing' gift from Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: Laura Woods couldn't contain her excitement over a thoughtful present from Mikel Arteta as she gears up for motherhood. The TNT Sport presenter, who announced her pregnancy with partner Adam Collard back in July and got engaged to him in September, took to Instagram to express her joy: "And just like that...

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Gunners set to lose star player as Vitor Reis price named
arts.sum: Arsenal are gearing up for the January transfer window, which opens in less than three weeks, though Gunners boss Mikel Arteta may be focused on outgoings rrather than reinforcements

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka identifies new Arsenal superstar: 'His future is very bright'
arts.sum: Myles Lewis-Skelly made his first Champions League start in the 3-0 win over Monaco on Wednesday night, having only previously started in the Carabao Cup, getting the nod ahead of Scotland international Kieran Tierney

arts.ttl: Sean Dyche insists Everton's TWELVE out-of-contract stars need to 'create noise' during Arsenal clash - as he insists that could provide 'an extra incentive' for Toffees
arts.sum: JOE BERNSTEIN: A dozen of Dyche's players will come to the end of their current deals at Goodison in the summer including striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin and Abdoulaye Doucoure.

arts.ttl: Lewis-Skelly unintentionally proved Mikel Arteta wrong after Arsenal performance stuns Monaco - Tom Canton
arts.sum: Three goals, none of which came from a set piece, meant that Mikel Arteta probably entered his post-match press conference with a sense of confidence he would avoid the increasingly grating questions about corners and free-kicks. In the end, however, Arsenal were able to come out on top and win with an element of comfort albeit the scoreline did flatter the Gunners.

arts.ttl: Fulham suffer Reiss Nelson injury blow as Marco Silva reveals recovery timeline
arts.sum: Fulham manager Marco Silva reveals the extent of Reiss Nelson's hamstring injury ahead of Saturday's Premier League meeting with Liverpool at Anfield.

arts.ttl: Man Utd fire subtle Arsenal dig with statement ahead of FA Cup clash
arts.sum: Manchester United put out a statement explaining why they had not put tickets on sale for their FA Cup clash at Arsenal pointing the finger at the Gunners for their failure to provide more information

arts.ttl: Gyokeres HUGE twist, Man Utd pick Garnacho over Rashford, Spurs eye Arsenal star's brother: FT Recap
arts.sum: Gyokeres HUGE twist, Man Utd pick Garnacho over Rashford, Spurs eye Arsenal star's brother: FT Recap The European matchweeks continued as teams prepare for the upcoming January transfer window ahead of a busy festive period.

arts.ttl: Why Man Utd, Liverpool, Arsenal and Barcelona aren't in the Club World Cup 2025
arts.sum: In Europe, Chelsea, Real Madrid (twice) and Manchester City are the teams that have won the Champions League since 2020/21, thus securing automatic qualification for the Club World Cup. 12 European sides will compete in the United States and their participation in the tournament is based on Champions League performances over the past four seasons, with all winners from 2020/21 to 2023/24 automatically reaching the group stage.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes Manchester City sack admission as Arsenal and Chelsea watch on
arts.sum: Ilkay Gundogan attributed the team's woes to low morale and a "mental issue" but Guardiola dismissed this notion post-match, maintaining that his squad performed well against Juventus. However, in a candid conversation with ex-Brescia colleague Luca Toni on Prime Video Sport, Guardiola opened up about the personal toll of City's downturn, saying: "Do Man City send me home?
